title: auth: use OsRng for challenge nonce, not thread_rng
description: core/src/auth.rs new_challenge() uses rand::thread_rng().fill() to generate the 32-byte challenge nonce. The signing key uses OsRng directly. In rand 0.8, thread_rng() is a ChaCha20 PRNG reseeded from OS entropy, not a direct OS RNG call. For a security-critical nonce that determines replay-attack resistance, use OsRng consistently. If thread_rng() fails to reseed after fork-without-exec, it degrades to a predictable stream.
